case_id: 256x256x129 T1-weighted MRI from Jeff Orchard's Brain Dataset

pi_items.source: task.json task description explicitly states 'minimum 20+ slices spanning the full range'. 256x256x129 stack supports at least 20 measurements.
For a 129-slice T1 brain MRI, tissue area varies dramatically from ~0 pixels at superior/inferior extremes to maximum in central slices. Conservative threshold appropriate for brain data.
For 256x256 images (max 65,536 pixels), central brain slices typically contain 10,000-40,000 pixels of thresholded tissue. 5000 pixels is reasonable minimum threshold.
256 × 256 = 65,536. This is the absolute theoretical maximum for any single slice in a 256x256 image.
Fiji/ImageJ menu: File › Open Samples › T1 Head (2.4M, 16-bits). Actual menu shows '16-bits' not 'MRI', and uses 'M' not 'MB'.

privileged_info_summary: The T1 Head sample is a real (non-synthetic) 256x256x129 pixel T1-weighted brain MRI scan from Jeff Orchard's dataset, originally sourced from UBC Hospital and BC Children's Hospital. In Fiji, it is accessed via File › Open Samples › T1 Head (2.4M, 16-bits). The image represents a complete anatomical brain scan from shoulder to top of head, with 129 sagittal slices. Brain tissue area varies significantly across slices (small near the extremes, maximum in central slices), suitable for volumetric analysis. The expected file output should contain at least 20 slice measurements with areas ranging from near 0 to approximately 5,000-40,000 pixels (well below the 65,536 pixel theoretical maximum for 256x256 images).
